description:
This is a pair of conceptual land cover maps illustrating a complex change event and the application of generalisation rules in land monitoring between 2012 and 2018. The left map, representing 2012, depicts several Land Cover / Land Use (LCLU) classes. Visible labelled classes include a large dark red polygon (LCLU class 1.3.1), two separate yellow polygons (LCLU class 4.1.0.0), and a light green polygon (LCLU class 3.1.1.0). Additional unlabelled background areas are shown in light blue, purple, and light yellow/cream. Cyan lines delineate the boundaries of the two yellow '4.1.0.0' polygons and a smaller adjacent polygon, all labelled as "„Areas related to change“".

The right map, representing 2018, shows the region after the change. The upper yellow '4.1.0.0' polygon persists, still highlighted by cyan lines. However, the lower '4.1.0.0' polygon and the small adjacent polygon from 2012 have been incorporated into an expanded dark red '1.3.1' LCLU polygon. The text box "„Areas related to change“" now points to the remaining yellow '4.1.0.0' polygon and the expanded '1.3.1' polygon which has subsumed the previously distinct smaller areas. This illustration demonstrates how areas below the Minimum Mapping Unit (MMU) undergoing change can be merged with neighbouring objects according to generalisation and priority rules, simplifying the land cover representation in datasets such as those from the Copernicus Land Monitoring Service (CLMS).
